description: The canonical registry of upstream spec surfaces the spec-drift watcher monitors for the kernel authoring family (@intentsolutions/core schemas/authoring/v1). One row per surface. Authored 2026-06-11. Companion docs: 000-docs/050-AT-SPEC-upstream-surface-registry-2026-06-11.md (the registry) + 000-docs/052-AT-SPEC-fetch-failure-taxonomy-and-three-tier-archive-2026-06-12.md (the per-surface `capture` config: fetch kind, expected extractor pattern, min-bytes floor). The byte-hash extractors that consume this list live in scripts/spec-drift-check.sh (today the SOURCES array is the executable copy; wiring the script to read this JSON directly is a tracked follow-up). The raw-fetch capture stage (scripts/fetch-capture.py) reads this JSON directly.
authority_precedence: official-spec-machine-readable  (e.g. MCP schema.ts) — exact field-level diff possible
official-spec                   (e.g. agentskills.io for skills, modelcontextprotocol.io for MCP) — the normative source
anthropic-doc                   (a Claude/Anthropic doc page ABOUT a spec) — beaten by the official spec it describes
reference                       (a Claude Code reference page — the spec for Claude-Code-only contracts that have no separate open standard)
release-feed                    (a GH releases.atom) — earliest material-change signal, ahead of any page edit
changelog                       (a CHANGELOG / npm version) — detects that change happened; the reference page is the spec

section_coverage_floor._why: Per-contract floors on how many page sections each extractor addresses BY NAME (scripts/section-coverage.py). Set 2026-07-23 at the then-measured values, so they ratchet: coverage may only grow silently, never shrink silently. These are floors, not targets — and they are deliberately LOW, because they record where extraction actually stands rather than where it should. Only 32 of 277 sections across the four heading-slice contracts are anchored, so the normative majority of every page (body prose, budgets, precedence chains, denylists, behaviour tables) is currently outside every projection and therefore invisible to FF#2. Raising a floor is the unit of progress here. Lowering one is a deliberate coverage reduction and must be reviewed in the same change. mcp-config is absent on purpose: it anchors on a sentence rather than a heading, so a heading count would be a category error, not a low score.

semantic_coverage_floor._why: A floor, not a target. Shrinking semantic coverage must be an explicit edit reviewed in the same change, because downstream 'the map got smaller' and 'the map is clean' are the same green. Raise it when a surface is promoted to field-level; lowering it is a deliberate coverage reduction.

semantic_coverage.note: Fitness Function #2. Also runs as its own dedicated watcher step, which is what drives the drift-issue / notification outputs; the freshness driver re-runs it so the coverage report is complete rather than partial.

semantic_coverage.reason: Declares contract skill-frontmatter but has NO extractor and NO committed projection of its own. FF#2's row regex is written against the agentskills.io frontmatter table specifically; this Anthropic page presents the same contract in a different shape. Covering it needs its own extractor + projection.
Release/commit atom feed. It signals THAT something changed, earlier than any page edit; it carries no normative field shape to project.

Deliberately frozen, and correctly so - this is NOT a repointing gap. The vendored mcp-schema-2025-11-25.json is the RELEASED 2025-11-25 revision, fetched from a pinned upstream commit (1304c8fe). This surface monitors schema/DRAFT/schema.ts, which is a different artifact on purpose: the draft is an early-change SIGNAL, the pinned release is the normative baseline. Repointing a pinned released schema at a moving draft would be a downgrade, not a repair. mcp-config's real freshness gap is its OTHER parsed input, the claude-code-mcp.md doc - see the claude-code-mcp-doc entry under unmonitored_candidates.
HTML spec docs; no extractor. The machine-readable schema is the higher authority for this contract.
Release atom feed — a change signal, not a normative shape.

semantic_coverage.note: Reconciled 2026-07-23: the reference doc was re-vendored from this capture, zero semantic findings.

semantic_coverage.reason: Vendored into the hook-config capture as supporting-doc only. It defines NO hook-handler fields, so the extractor does not consume it and there is nothing to project. Kept monitored because a change there can still move hooks-block placement.

CORRECTED 2026-07-23 (supersedes the contract-#7 framing this entry previously carried). The URL still reads slash-commands.md, but upstream WHOLLY REPLACED the document: it now serves the H1 'Extend Claude with skills' and its subject is SKILL.md. Verified against a raw fetch, and the committed snapshot is byte-identical to it - so the capture already pulled the replacement and nothing flagged it, because expect_regex '(?m)^#{1,3} ' matches any heading on any page. That is the FETCH_OK-on-a-replaced-page hole, tracked separately via capture.expect_title. The page states 'Custom commands have been merged into skills' and that .claude/commands/ files 'support the same frontmatter'. The contract governing this surface is therefore skill-frontmatter - kernel contract #1, already PUBLISHED - and NOT a prospective #7. There is nothing to charter here: the previous entry reasoned from the URL and the surface name rather than from the document, and concluded a charter decision was open when the artifact class had simply been folded into an existing contract. What remains true and unfixed: claude-code-plugins' validate-skills-schema.py grades commands/*.md against its own hand-rolled category list rather than the kernel's skill-frontmatter schema. That is a consumer-conformance gap on contract #1, not a missing contract. Coverage stays byte-hash-only because this page presents skill-frontmatter in a different shape than the agentskills.io frontmatter table FF#2's row regex is written against - the same reason platform-skills-overview is byte-hash-only - so covering it needs its own extractor and projection.

semantic_coverage.note: Reconciled 2026-07-23: the reference doc was re-vendored from this capture, zero semantic findings.

RECONCILED 2026-07-23. The finding was frontmatter.fields.permissionMode.enum disappearing, and it was NOT a moved anchor: upstream added a 7th value `manual` (an alias for `default`, Claude Code v2.1.200+) phrased with an MDX min-version comment and the words 'as an alias for', which turned a closed-set value clause into an open one. The closed-set-only extractor correctly declined an enum - but the projection then recorded NOTHING for that field, rendering a value ADDITION as a bare absence. Fixed by recording open-clause tokens under enum_candidates + enum_clause_open (the split the projection already made for `model` via model_full_id_latitude), so `enum` stays honest about closedness while the values survive. Two compounding parse defects were found and fixed getting there: the clause stop truncated INSIDE the version number '2.1.200' and silently dropped the trailing value, and an alias clause reported `default` twice - either alone yields a plausible, wrong candidate list. Both are pinned by fixtures in the extractor self-test. The reference doc is re-vendored from this capture and --check-fresh is clean, so this surface is now ENFORCED. No kernel edit implied: permissionMode is an EXPECTED_DIVERGENCE (doc-fields-not-in-kernel).

RECONCILED 2026-07-23, and the last of the five field-level surfaces to become ENFORCED. All four findings were verified against the captured page before the re-vendor, not assumed: (1) catalog.renames - object, former-name to current-name migration map, Claude Code v2.1.193+; (2) plugin_entry.relevance - object, org-allowlisted plugin suggestion signals, v2.1.152+, which also joins the documented marketplace_specific_fields list; (3) catalog.reserved_names gains `first-party-plugins` and `healthcare`. Each has its own table row AND its own doc section on the captured page and is absent from the frozen one. (3) is MATERIAL, not bookkeeping: the page now states Claude Code re-checks reserved names on EVERY marketplace load rather than only on add, so a marketplace already registered under a newly-reserved name STOPS LOADING and reports an untrusted source (before v2.1.205 it kept loading). Estate exposure was checked, not assumed: no marketplace.json under the projects tree uses either new name; the IS marketplace is `claude-code-plugins-plus`, which is not reserved but IS a suffix-extension of the reserved `claude-code-plugins`, and the same paragraph blocks impersonating names by prose judgement - a watch item, not a break. NO kernel authoring/v1 edit is implied: upstream-base/marketplace-catalog.v1.json is a required-set FLOOR that enumerates no optional plugin-entry fields and leaves additionalProperties permissive, so both new fields already validate and adding them would be cosmetic. The extractor's pinned self-test expectations moved with the re-vendor (8->9 top-level, 14->16 reserved, 20->21 entry fields, and the two fields joining doc-fields-not-in-kernel) - those sets exist to fail LOUD on a re-capture so a human reconciles, which is what happened.

semantic_coverage.reason: version-signal contract: detects that a release happened. The reference pages are the spec; there is no shape here to project.
version-signal contract: the published npm version string. No shape to project.
version-signal contract: release atom feed. No shape to project.
cross-cutting-signal contract: narrative engineering posts, deliberately not a normative surface. Byte-hash is the whole intended coverage.

unmonitored_candidates.name: claude-plugins-official
unmonitored_candidates.reason: marketplace.json manifests; the plugin-marketplaces.md reference page is the spec and IS monitored. Add the repo's marketplace manifest if a sampleable raw file is identified (Wave 3).

unmonitored_candidates.name: claude-code-mcp-doc
unmonitored_candidates.reason: Parsed by extract-mcp-projection.py (vendored as claude-code-mcp.md with surface null) but NOT a registered surface, so that half of the mcp-config capture cannot be repointed at a captured snapshot and mcp-config stays byte-hash-only. Registering it is the prerequisite for field-level mcp-config coverage.
